description: Places-readiness spot-check list (10 ZIPs). Prepared for a controlled, budgeted future Places test. Does NOT run Places by itself. Max 40 live calls (10 ZIPs x 4 nearby_search queries). ~5 of these are already in the 71-ZIP enriched cache, so with --refresh-days 30 expect ~20 live calls and ~20 cache hits. Outliers 07514 and 47809 are excluded by the offline api-candidate gate; pass --disable-api-filter to force-enrich them.

zips.reason: #1 modeled overall (76.2) and #1 CPR tilt (87.5); Hoboken NJ, very dense urban (47k/sq mi). Validate that Places competitor/hospital counts match a true top finalist.
NYC Murray Hill, overall 75.7, highest density in the set (99k/sq mi), NYU Langone medical district. Stress-tests Places behavior at the 20-result search cap (saturation check).
Sunnyvale CA, overall 75.4, highest offline api-candidate score (48.2). Already enriched -> expected cache hit; good cache-vs-live regression check.
Brooklyn Park Slope, overall 75.3, largest population (72k). Dense residential -> community/CPR demand archetype.
SF Mission, overall 75.0, dense (29k). Already enriched -> expected cache hit.
Fremont CA, overall 65, suburban density (4003). Part of prior Bay Area Places validation; expected cache hit.
Hayward CA, overall 61.1. The demo example-search ZIP; confirm detail panel + enrichment render. Expected cache hit.
San Jose CA, overall 61.9, has real ALLCPR class history. Lets us compare live Places enrichment against proven demand (calibration). Expected cache hit.

Paterson NJ, very dense (19,893/sq mi) but weak modeled demand (overall 39.8). Gate-EXCLUDED (overall < 45). Tests whether Places reveals hidden healthcare/competitor signal the public model under-weights. Use --disable-api-filter.

Terre Haute IN (Indiana State University), high density (11,000) but tiny resident population (1,716). Gate-EXCLUDED (pop < 5000). Tests a university-campus false-density case. Use --disable-api-filter.
